caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The supplied 5413 role mix is engineering-dominated rather than 541320-specific.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Survey use is task- and respondent-based, not a measured labor-hour replacement study.
inputs.pi_dist: ASLA contract forms demonstrate relationship types, not fee method, price realization or revenue shares.
inputs.pi_moat: No accessible source in this packet quantifies 541320 revenue inside the regulated practice perimeter.
inputs.t50_years: No landscape-specific longitudinal effective-adoption series was located; this is not derived from a CAD/BIM timeline.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: The ASLA survey is not a census of US 541320 firms and its time-saved measure is respondent-reported.
inputs.historical_analogs: No dated landscape-specific CAD/BIM adoption observations were reopened.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: Zero is a scoring-conservative placeholder, not a claim that no owners are over 60.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: Absence of evidence in this packet is not evidence of no succession issue.

inputs.active_consolidators: The announcements do not disclose target EBITDA, consideration, recurring-platform strategy or PE backing for every buyer; only Populous' separate PE minority investment is verified in S8.
inputs.buy_mult: ROG covers mixed architecture, engineering and environmental transactions, including internal minority interests; it is not a 541320 target-comps series.
inputs.exit_mult: No cited landscape-architecture transaction reports a paid multiple or establishes an eligible $10M-plus EBITDA platform exit.
inputs.pricing_structure: No accessible LA-specific billing or prime/sub-consultant revenue-share survey was reopened.
inputs.active_consolidators: The value 3 is a cross-source count of three announcements, not a directly reported market-wide consolidator statistic.
inputs.exit_mult: ROG's 5.38x figure is the post-IIJA all-firms median, not the upper endpoint of an all-firms 3.35x-5.38x range; the all-firms lower quartile and median are 3.35x and 5.30x.

q.rationale: The existing v3 distribution-retention × moat-retention judgment is reused as a bounded Stage 1 commercial-retention proxy.

leaves.rationale: Predeclared bounded demand/operator row for the inherited v3 terminal class; unknown classes remain missing.

e.rationale: Global eligible-union share; heterogeneous records use the predeclared wider/lower class-count row.

n.detail: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[54]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $206,548 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 541320); class <5: 3,549 firms, avg revenue/firm $379,051 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $59,321 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 800 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,193,496 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $186,782 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 390 firms, avg revenue/firm $2,621,638 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $410,286 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 256 firms, avg revenue/firm $6,121,246 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $957,975 -> out of $1-10M band; class 100-499: 33 firms, avg revenue/firm $23,646,333 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $3,700,651 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 13 firms, avg revenue/firm $9,128,077 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $1,428,544 -> IN $1-10M band; n_band = 46

s5.rationale: Fixed transform of the disclosed v3 owner-age range into five-year qualifying control-transfer propensity.

a.rationale: Observed use is concentrated in early design, reports and RFPs, so production/documentation roles receive partial rather than majority automation fractions; accountable field and management work remains low. The 4-digit occupation mix is a disclosed proxy.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 541320 ($2,653,798,071) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 541320 ($5,788,521,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

rho.rationale: Fixed outcome-blind transform of the v3 t50 adoption estimate, widened by the declared bridge error.

source_review.summary: Publishable with caveats. Exact schema, identity, model route, frozen digests, dataset injection, fresh finalization, memo rendering, and arithmetic all pass. All ten registered sources reopened and support the directional task, adoption, licensure, relationship, transaction, and valuation evidence. The kill thesis remains useful and no factor semantics or material terminal/capture double count is reversed; publication must preserve the authored proxy limitations plus the two non-fatal provenance and ROG-statistic corrections.

sources.title: How Landscape Architects Are Incorporating Artificial Intelligence
sources.what_it_supports: ASLA's 2024 survey reports the respondent mix, 55% any AI use, task uses, 27% reporting time saved, and 15% of firms allowing billable experimentation.

sources.title: 2025 AI in Landscape Architecture Survey
sources.what_it_supports: IFLA's global landscape-architecture survey reports AI use concentrated in research, writing and predesign, plus efficiency and governance findings.

sources.title: ASLA: Will I Need a License?
sources.what_it_supports: ASLA's accessible current page distinguishes title acts from practice acts and states that all 50 states license landscape architects.

sources.title: CLARB Glossary of Terms
sources.what_it_supports: CLARB defines practice acts and explains practice overlap and incidental practice among related design professions.

sources.title: ASLA Standard Form Contracts for Professional Services
sources.what_it_supports: ASLA offers distinct landscape architect-client and landscape architect-consultant forms, supporting the existence of both relationship types but not their revenue shares.

sources.title: Greenprint Acquires Omni Workshop, Landscape Architecture Studio
sources.what_it_supports: Greenprint announced its January 2025 acquisition of Omni Workshop, described as a full-service landscape architecture and design studio.

sources.title: ISG Acquires Lift Environmental Design
sources.what_it_supports: ISG announced its July 2025 acquisition of Lift Environmental Design, a landscape architecture and planning firm.

sources.title: Populous Announces Minority Investment from Providence Equity Partners
sources.what_it_supports: Populous disclosed a 2023 strategic minority investment from Providence Equity Partners and an intent to expand its services and geography.

sources.title: Populous Acquires OJB Landscape Architecture
sources.what_it_supports: Populous announced its May 2026 acquisition of OJB Landscape Architecture; the announcement identifies landscape architecture, urban design and public-realm capabilities but discloses no consideration or EBITDA.

sources.title: ROG: Latest A/E Business Valuation and M&A Transaction Study
sources.what_it_supports: ROG reports 257 mixed architecture, engineering and environmental transactions and their all-firms EV/EBITDA distribution; it is an explicitly non-landscape-specific valuation reference.

title: Landscape Architectural Services
